valid.name: case-folded
valid.code: CREATE TABLE UserAccounts (id INT PRIMARY KEY);

valid.name: snake
valid.code: CREATE TABLE user_accounts (id INT PRIMARY KEY);

errors.message: Table name `UserAccounts` is not snake_case. PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lower case but preserves the case of quoted identifiers; mixing the two leads to `relation "BadName" does not exist` errors that are confusing to debug.
